code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test22-SEP-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urinalysis shows glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance, but no evidence of infection. Blood glucose and electrolytes are within normal limits, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or altered red cell turnover. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and renal impairment.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
